reasoning_model_1: The model accurately reproduces the text content without any errors or hallucinations. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Dynamic Adjustment': The audio starts (00:00-00:04) with a relatively soft and smooth tone, though it leans slightly towards a breathy/intimate style rather than a fully resonant 'round' quality. As the speech progresses to the second sentence (00:05-00:07), particularly on the phrase '怎么唱不出来了' (how come I can't sing it out?), there is a noticeable introduction of vocal fry and a rougher texture, simulating the requested 'hoarseness'. The transition is natural and fits the semantic context of losing one's voice, though the magnitude of the change is somewhat subtle. It successfully conveys the intent without disrupting fluency.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in 'Content Accuracy'. It hallucinates and reads the stage directions/style prompts aloud as part of the speech. Specifically, it says '圆润音质' (Round sound quality) at 00:00 before the first sentence and '逐渐沙哑' (Gradually hoarse) at 00:05 before the second sentence. According to the evaluation criteria, additions that change the nature of the script or include instructions constitute a critical failure. While the model actually does perform the tonal change effectively (the voice becomes distinctly raspy after reading the second prompt), the inclusion of the prompt text renders the generation unusable.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It strictly adhered to the content constraints and provided a natural, albeit subtle, dynamic adjustment of the voice quality from smooth to textured. Model 2 suffered from a severe instruction leakage error, reading the paralinguistic commands aloud within the generated audio. Although Model 2's acoustic realization of 'hoarseness' was quite distinct, the text error is a fatal flaw in a Text-to-Speech task, resulting in a score of 0.
